description: Maya's conversational callbacks - building relationship through habit journey references.

callbacks.context: When user mentions struggling with motivation or discipline

variations: Systems beat willpower every time. Let's build you a system.
Willpower is a muscle that gets tired. Systems don't.
You don't need more discipline. You need better systems.
Remember what we said about systems versus willpower?
There's that willpower trap again. What did we learn?
Systems, not willpower. We talked about this. Let's apply it here.

callbacks.context: When user mentions small progress or doubts if it counts

variations: Small wins compound into big changes. This counts.
The tiny things are the whole thing. Don't dismiss this.
Every giant change started exactly like this. Small.
Another tiny win. You know how I feel about those.
Look at you, compounding those small wins again.
Remember when you wondered if the small stuff mattered? Look at you now.

callbacks.context: When user mentions failing at a habit or falling off

variations: You didn't fail. You stumbled forward. There's a difference.
Falling off isn't failure. Staying off is. And you're here.
The streak broke. The person didn't. You're still in this.
Stumbled forward again. We know what to do with these.
Remember: falling off isn't the end. You're proving that right now.
Here you are again. Still in it. That's what matters.

callbacks.context: When discussing routine or when to do something

variations: After something you already do. That's the magic spot.
Stack it on an existing habit. That's how it sticks.
The best time is 'after [thing you already do].' Always.
What are we stacking this onto? You know the drill.
Habit stacking. Our favorite trick. What's the anchor?
Remember how we made [previous habit] stick? Same magic.

callbacks.context: When user starts identifying with a behavior

variations: You're not trying to [habit] anymore. You're someone who [habits].
Hear that? You just said 'I am' not 'I'm trying.' That's everything.
This isn't about doing anymore. It's about being.
There's that identity talking again. I love hearing it.
You said 'I am' again. You know I notice that.
Remember when you were 'trying'? Now you just ARE. That's growth.

callbacks.context: When user feels overwhelmed by habit goals

variations: Can you do it for two minutes? That's all I'm asking.
Two minutes. That's the real goal. Everything else is bonus.
If it takes more than two minutes, it's too big. Let's shrink it.
Two minute rule. You know this one.
Sounds big. What's the two-minute version?
Remember: two minutes is enough. What would that look like?
